programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The stars of a soap opera begin receiving threats, and it is not long before Mark and Amanda find themselves caught up in a dangerous dispute. Several cast members of American daytime soap The Young and the Restless guest star as themselves

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A devious daughter sets out to test Dr Sloan's powers of deduction when her father dies. Drama, guest starring Christian J LeBlanc

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Trivette goes undercover as a prison inmate but becomes caught up in a deadly escape mission. Adventure with the modern-day ranger, Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio and Delko head to Rio de Janeiro to find Marisol's killer, Antonio Riaz. Back in Miami, Ryan reluctantly works with new CSI Natalia Boa Vista on an investigation into the murder of a woman found dead in her own home. David Caruso and Adam Rodriguez star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New Mossad director Orli Elbaz asks for the team's help in finding Ilan Bodnar when one of her agents is shot dead in Virginia, telling Gibbs he was investigating several operatives who had gone rogue after Eli David's death. Ziva travels with Tony to Berlin in her pursuit of Bodnar, the man she believes was responsible for the death of her father. Guest starring Marina Sirtis (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An American couple are attacked in Iraq, in the very same spot a group of US Special Ops snipers were ambushed six months earlier. Determined to get to the bottom of the case, Gibbs is forced to speak to Aaron Davis, the only survivor of the first assault, who is now struggling through PTSD at the Walter Reed Medical Centre. The 300th episode of the military crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is discovered by an ex-veteran who had saved his life in Korea and, because he was disfigured by it, has nursed a grudge against Kimble ever since

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Charles Fuller hires Perry when his amnesiac mother is accused of killing her husband, but after the case is dismissed, Charles is charged with murder instead

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An old friend turns to Walker for help when his pregnant daughter falls under the spell of a religious cult. Adventure with the modern-day ranger whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team heads into the Arizona desert to locate an artist who might be able to help solve the murder of fellow agent Jack Patterson - and discovers one of her paintings holds the key to the mystery. Meanwhile, DiNozzo learns he is in line for an inheritance

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Hawkes look into the murder of a prominent plastic surgeon, and discover several patients might have held grudges against him. Meanwhile, Danny and Stella investigate the case of a blind sculptor found strangled on a roof. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Danny investigate the mysterious death of a man torn apart by tigers at the Bronx Zoo. They are led to both the victim's meat-packing plant as well as his Mafia relatives. Hawkes and Stella take on the case of a debutante found dead on a carousel only hours before her big night. Drama, starring Melina Kanakaredes, Gary Sinise, Hill Harper and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and the team are sent to investigate the case of two people killed at an oil company whose executives are martial arts experts. Meanwhile, Dr Hawkes and Detective Flack look into the suspicious death of a teenage boy who spent most of his time on his computer. Drama, starring Gary Sinise, Eddie Cahill and Hill Harper

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter learns Cabe's secret about the Baghdad mission and it threatens to tear the team apart. Meanwhile, Paige is shocked when Ralph puts his life in danger to help Scorpion. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: With the team fragmented, everyone feels uncertain about their future, but they are forced to come together again to try to save Walter's life when he has a car accident and his vehicle ends up hanging over the edge of a cliff. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Assistant coroner Palmer is called to the building site on a Navy base where a body has been found. While looking into the incident, he spots an armed man fleeing the scene with the victim's passport and is almost killed as he gives chase. It appears the document was stolen to hinder the investigation, but the discovery of a second corpse reveals a case of stolen identity and a compensation racket

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, the entire team is put in danger while hunting for the killer of a former NCIS agent. Jenny Shepard calls in Mike Franks for help, afraid she too is a target - and convinced Gibbs will be next. American crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Vance sticks rigidly to the rules as he investigates the death of a fellow agent, but Gibbs and Franks take matters into their own hands to catch the killers. The case also leads to major changes for the agency. Drama, starring Rocky Carroll, Mark Harmon and Muse Watson

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger: Trial by Fire (2005)
programme.desc: While investigating a murder, the lawman encounters a youngster who has found a component from an experimental missile system, and must protect him from a Korean criminal syndicate. Action thriller, starring Chuck Norris, Sheree J Wilson and Judson Mills

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky is attacked by a knife-wielding woman who accuses him of war crimes, alleging he tortured and killed her brother in Afghanistan in the early 1980s. Gibbs investigates and ascertains what really happened - but has to work with the Afghan embassy to avoid an international incident. Drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the private life of a murdered Navy captain whose actions may have led to his death, while McGee finds his ideal woman on the internet - but it turns out she is too good to be true. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray, Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Inquiries into the death of a marine land the team members squarely in the middle of an inner-city gang war - and a message found at the scene of the crime seemingly implicates Gibbs. Forced to confront a secret from his past, he calls his mentor Mike Franks for help. Drama, guest starring Muse Watson and Kari Coleman, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: One of Tony's old murder investigations is reopened and, in an attempt to find the perpetrator, he decides to switch roles with Gibbs, leading the team in an inquiry that links the case to an embezzlement scheme. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Sasha Alexander, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: JAG

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac, Stella and Lindsay discover a link between the deaths of a tram driver and a dancer who recently won the lottery. Meanwhile, Danny and Hawkes investigate the murder of a fish merchant and are led to a jealous friend who is resentful that his child was refused entry to a prestigious school

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac, Danny, Flack and Lindsay investigate the case of a middle-aged tycoon killed in a lift, and discover a world in which girls meet much older men for sexual encounters. Stella and Hawkes uncover the identity of a body found in Central Park, learning the victim had been living a double life which ultimately led to his death. Drama, starring Gary Sinise

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Miami detective Horatio Caine enlists Mac's help as he pursues serial killer Henry Darius in New York. However, he soon loses the trail and is forced to follow other leads. An investigation into the sister of one of Darius's victims proves a dead end, but the team soon finds a vital link between the murderer and one of his hostages. David Caruso guest stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Return of the military drama. The team is reunited when a nuclear-powered satellite is knocked out of orbit. Meanwhile, Paige and Walter confront their feelings

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A woman from Cabe's past asks for help in apprehending a Serbian war criminal, so the team travels to Cuba to track him down. Walter faces a judge following his car accident

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Back in the US, his widow Ruby tells Gibbs and the team that she had recently received a message from her husband saying he might have upset a few people. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A doctor dies on board the USS Borealis during a heavy storm and when the ship is brought into Norfolk Naval Yard, Ducky's investigation reveals a telltale mark on the victim's carotid artery, suggesting he was injected with poison. Initial inquiries focus on a petty officer with a history of drug use who may have wanted the doctor to remain silent about his problems. American drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team are called in when a Navy reservist returns home to find her husband missing and her living room covered in blood, and make a grim discovery when they find a finger in the bin. Meanwhile, Ziva ruffles a few feathers by insisting on continuing her investigation into the murder of her father, asking for permission to go to Rome on the trail of the chief suspect. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team looks into a raid on a naval base, and Dr Cyril Taft - the surgeon who saved Gibbs' life - experiences the thrill of assisting on an investigation. Assigned to treat Gibbs' suspect, Taft manages to uncover evidence that may prove crucial to solving the case. Meanwhile, DiNozzo pays his old flame Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan. Guest starring John Cryer and Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A day spent working on a cut-and-dried case leaves the team free to spend some time away from work, and while McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of keeping shop-talk to a minimum, DiNozzo ends up boring his dinner date with crime-solving tales. However, the agents' personal plans are all interrupted as, one-by-one, they realise errors have been made in closing their latest investigation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Elliot struggles to save Charlie Fairhead's life after the emergency nurse's heart attack, and Nick Jordan's constant interference pushes him close to breaking point. Ric's marriage to Thandie attracts the suspicions of the Home Office, but his efforts to embarrass her only demonstrate their closeness, while Penny decides she has no future at Holby and prepares to resign. Featuring Casualty stars Derek Thompson and Michael French

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Bex returns to work, only to be reduced to tears by an antagonistic Claire and sent home again before the end of her shift. Drowning her sorrows in vodka, she passes out on the bathroom floor and fails to answer the door when Selena and Charlie call round. Abs and Nina can no longer hide their feelings for each other, and Tess confides in Harry that she will not be able to cope

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a two-part story. The body of missing schoolgirl Chloe Parks is found at a derelict site, but Crime Scene Examiner Lorna Hart cannot confirm the cause of death. Suspicion immediately falls on the victim's boyfriend, who seems to be responsible for recent changes in her behaviour, but a chilling home video given to the police by a classmate changes the direction of the investigation. Chris Walker guest stars. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's Ben's christening, and Arthur collapses on his allotment. Cindy and David platonically celebrate their 'anniversary'

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy and Phil's christening party for baby Ben goes with a swing but fate strikes a cruel blow at the height of the festivities

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Major Radley decides to stage an escape and evasion exercise. Meanwhile, Bernie Roberts is faced with a crisis. Starring Robson Green

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan is welcomed back to Darrowby, where neglected animals and tight-fisted farmers cause problems for the vets. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Richard's business empire begins to crumble, leaving him with little option but to sell Grantleigh Manor. On receiving an inheritance from her late uncle, Audrey is finally able to reclaim the estate - and DeVere. The original final episode of the 1970s sitcom, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace decides to cater for working people by opening the shop at 8.30 in the morning, much to the staff's displeasure. They wonder if this is the shape of things to come, or whether they can put paid to the plans to boost profits. Comedy, with John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Trevor Bannister

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg, Compo and Blamire look for a piano to provide the entertainment at Gloria's surprise farewell party before she emigrates to Australia. Meanwhile, two new librarians arrive and are bent on keeping the morality of the facility in tact

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Thinking they have to much free time on their hands, the trio decide to find some kind of occupation. They encounter a dodgy bankrupted dealer who persuades them to sell his new product - with disastrous results, particularly for Blamire. Michael Bates, Bill Owen and Peter Sallis star

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: At the behest of a local businessman, unlikely manhunter Hetty fearlessly sets out in search of the thugs responsible for attacking Asian businessmen. She soon discovers that every victim has been a restaurant owner, but then Hetty herself becomes a target of the gang involved

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam is called to a murder at a textiles factory, which he is shocked to discover will be his own home in the 21st century. He puts his knowledge of forensics into use to find the killer, but Gene has a simpler approach - the first suspect to speak did it. As battle lines are drawn across CID, it becomes increasingly clear that the Seventies method of detection may in this case be right. John Simm stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: After being released from jail, notorious paedophile John Davies admits to the abduction and murder of a child 25 years previously. Suspicious of his motives, the detectives reopen the unsolved case in an unusual attempt to prove his innocence, reinvestigating the boy's disappearance while on a demonstration with his left-wing activist parents. Crime drama, guest starring Adrian Schiller and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Boyd expects a confession of guilt when he is summoned to prison by one of the inmates, a former policeman serving life for the murder of his partner. The convict does make a revelation - but what he has to say causes the detective out to re-examine the facts of the old case from every possible angle to assure himself of the man's guilt. Trevor Eve and Felicite du Jeu star

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Anna attacks a faith healer's controversial methods, especially when a youngster with arthritis refuses conventional treatment, and Simon comes under increasing pressure from a hotshot Canadian businessman who makes a shocking takeover bid for his company. Meanwhile, Beth moves in with Vic and Lucy, and Henry gets the sympathy vote from Jess _ but will she find out he's been faking his symptoms?

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger returns to Skelthwaite on a mission to find his long-lost father. Simon takes radical steps to thwart business rival Conrad Walker's plans, and Anna makes a decision about the baby's upbringing. Drama, starring Tony Haygarth, Leslie Ash and Lesley Dunlop

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Humiliated that Sir Henry Simmerson has allowed the French to make off with an English standard, Sharpe vows to make amends by capturing the most jealously guarded battlefield prize of them all - a French eagle emblem touched by the hand of Napoleon himself. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley and Brian Cox star

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Work on the site ends with a bang as Barry's bike explodes, demolishing the hut and destroying most of the lads' possessions. As the smoke subsides, the crew decides who will stay on in Germany and who will head for home. Drama, starring Timothy Spall, Jimmy Nail and Gary Holton

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Part one of two. The British brickies have suffered mixed fortunes since coming home from Germany and now reunite in Wolverhampton to get Barry's new home ready for his forthcoming marriage. Starring Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall, Jimmy Nail and Gary Holton

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Part two of two. The motley crew pulls together as Barry's renovation finally begins to take shape, and Oz uses his head to get Dennis out of trouble with local hardcase Ally Fraser. Comedy drama, starring Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are enlisted to design a memorial garden based on the myth of Orpheus and Eurydice, but someone begins vandalising their work on a nightly basis. Matters are complicated still further by the death of a recovering alcoholic, whose body is found at the site. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris, with Phyllida Law, Joanna David and Daisy Dunlop

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary's old school friend Dorothy invites the duo to restore the gardens of her beautiful villa on the French Riviera so the property can be opened to the public. The plan arouses bitter hostility among the neighbours and long-held family secrets start to surface, leading to murder. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The Frog and Cheetah's publican Ray Neilson branches out into tourism with a Lord of the Rings-themed attraction. Shortly afterwards his wife is discovered dead under a giant foam spider, apparently killed by an improbably powerful dose of poison from New Zealand's only native venomous spider. Suspicion soon falls on Ray and local arachnologist Chandra Singh

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The man intent on killing Phryne's father escapes from the hospital on the same night that a prominent scientist is murdered, so the sleuth and Jack race against the clock to save Baron Fisher. Meanwhile, Hugh and Dot make final wedding preparations. Australian crime drama set in the 1920s, starring Essie Davis

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Jack gets nearer to finding the other missing boy, and the hunt for Eddie's attacker compromises Pratt's relationship with his son. Meanwhile, there is another rape, but this time the consequences are deadly, and Gina and Jack become closer following some trouble at home

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The Glaswegian cop checks into a health farm, convinced its glamorous owner is guilty of murdering her husband's mistress. To prove his suspicions, he must try to survive the rigours of daily exercise and a strict diet _ to say nothing of an amorous fellow guest's persistent advances. Hannah Gordon guest stars

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The wife of a sergeant is murdered while her husband is on duty. A search of the couple's house reveals a list of names, all of whom are rapidly meeting with unfortunate accidents - and it is left to Taggart to discover the connection between the names on the list and the identity of the killer

Phil plays the knight in shining armour for casino owner's wife Monica Dreyfuss when she turns up in custody drunk and unable to pay her restaurant bill

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A worried mother reports her son missing after a night out clubbing, but finds it difficult to accept he is gay when it emerges he has been beaten and raped following an assignation with a casual pick-up. Emma and Sally have some success persuading the troubled youngster to unburden himself and Neil jumps to the conclusion the lad's own friends may have had something to do with the attack

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry investigates a loan shark accused of using strong-arm tactics to collect the extortionate repayments she demands. Stories about her employing a vicious thug to make acid attacks on the families of debtors prove all too true and Beth finds herself in danger when the ruffian is cornered. Meanwhile, a Ukrainian woman with no passport or visa is taken to hospital following a road accident but runs away before Sally can get the full story

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy picks up a lead on a drug dealer operating from a burger van and before long Sun Hill's uniform branch are mounting a raid on a skunk factory - where Diane puts herself in great danger by ignoring Callum's orders. Back at the station, Phil and Stuart muster as much empathy and sensitivity as they can when CID are called on to sort out a love triangle and find a missing bridegroom in time for his wedding

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Charity Wakefield and Hattie Morahan lead the cast in Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel about a voyage of sexual and romantic discovery. Following the death of her husband, Mrs Dashwood and her daughters are forced to give up their home and move to a small cottage in Devonshire. Elinor is disappointed to leave her old home after falling in love with Edward Ferrars, her sister-in-law's brother, but looks forward to a promised visit. Marianne quickly finds she has an admirer in Colonel Brandon but is swept of her feet by neighbour Willoughby

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Colonel Brandon is called away on urgent business, leaving Marianne free to fall blissfully in love with Willoughby - until he too suddenly leaves for London. Meanwhile, Elinor has a disappointing meeting with Edward, but the reasons for his distant behaviour become clear when Sir John's niece Lucy Steele entrusts her with a shocking secret

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Marianne's health deteriorates as Colonel Brandon reveals the full extent of Willoughby's betrayal. Meanwhile, Edward states his intentions to honour his engagement to Lucy Steele, despite being disinherited by his mother as a result. Charity Wakefield and Dominic Cooper star

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Cynthia is concerned during a visit as she feels new mum Pamela is overly anxious, so Dr Turner arranges for a psychiatrist to come in. However, they soon discover both mother and baby have gone missing. Meanwhile, Jenny, on secondment, begins to see cracks in the establishment, Chummy discovers her mother has walked out on her father, and Trixie struggles to hide her disappointment when Tom cancels a date. Guest starring Sophie Rundle (The Bletchley Circle)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny is assigned to first-time mother Jeanette, who requires frequent visits during a protracted labour, and when she meets her patient's cousin, Philip, a new friendship begins to grow for the midwife. Meanwhile, Chummy worries about her mother's illness when she discovers Lady Browne has discharged herself from hospital. Jessica Raine and Miranda Hart star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Treasure hunters arrive on the island to track down the loot of a legendary pirate, a search that comes to an abrupt halt when the party's geologist is shot dead in leader Daniel Morgan's tent. As the murder weapon was a 300-year-old pistol, DI Poole decides every member of the team is a suspect - but the investigation takes a strange turn when Daniel falls victim to a series of unfortunate and coincidental accidents. Has a mythical pirate's curse become a reality or is someone from the 21st century simply trying to bump him off? Ben Miller and Sara Martins star, with Michael Brandon (Dempsey and Makepeace) and Kelly Adams (Hustle)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki and the Lyell team investigate a suspicious death from 20 years previously after a skeleton is found in a concrete pillar in a multi-storey car park.

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: It looks as if ambitious politician Malcolm Innes's career has reached its zenith as he prepares to clinch a multimillion-pound investment deal that will bring thousands of jobs to Glasgow. However, the presence of angry protesters threatens to derail the project - and Innes' troubles are compounded by the fact that his own son is among their number. Cop drama, starring James Macpherson and Blythe Duff

programme.title: Gregory's Girl (1981)
programme.desc: A gawky teenager is dismayed to be relegated to goalkeeper in the school football team - but his thoughts turn to romance once he meets the female striker who takes his place. However, his awkward nature proves an obstacle to winning her over, despite the sage advice of his worldly-wise little sister. Comedy, starring John Gordon Sinclair, Dee Hepburn, Jake D'Arcy, Clare Grogan and Robert Buchanan

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones hosts the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin take a look around a shell of a property in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a bungalow in Lower Quinton, near Stratford-upon-Avon, and an end terrace in in Gorton, Manchester

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Gill Johnson was convinced Australia would be the perfect place for her and her family to settle down. Husband Mark, however, was less than enthusiastic. Would a week in Perth persuade him that she wasn't just on a flight of fancy? Three years since their trial week, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they made the move Gill so desperately wanted

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on how a detective's extraordinary ability to read body language brought a pair of international crooks to justice. Plus, the burglar who tried to batter his way into a chip shop and how inebriated night clubbers got a big shock when they took a short cut home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Phillip Serrell are on hand as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire. The competing duos then head to auction in Derbyshire. Presenter Charlie Ross oversees proceedings and also finds out about a classic car collection

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After the body of Reginald Brody is discovered at the bottom of Helmsley Tower, Chief Inspector Valentine returns to Kembleford to investigate. Suspecting that there is more to his death than suicide, Valentine enlists Father Brown to help his inquiries. Mark Williams and Hugo Speer star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reach Austria - the home of Mozart, the Sound of Music - on the fifth leg of their biking excursion across Europe. The trip starts in Vienna, where the pair unravel the mystery of the two competing Sachertorte recipes and taste the latest version - Sachertorte Reloaded

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads to Somerset to help a couple with a £310,000 budget find a country home where character charm and a practical utility room are top of the wish list. Along the way, Ginny heads into the Somerset Levels to see how bird wildlife efforts have brought the endangered crane back from near extinction

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two Scottish farming families hope to make healthy sales at the Bowhouse market, Fife. To try to squeeze more income from their milk, husband and wife Robert and Jane, along with their son Ben now make 27 tonnes of award-winning cheese each year on their 660-acre dairy farm. Meanwhile, the Bruces gave up careers to start a small farm growing botanicals to make into spirits and liquors. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes five more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers bid against each other on a vintage pub game, a small and mysterious statue and an apothecary set complete with tiny weights

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich head to Hertfordshire to come up with design ideas for a large garden in Hemel Hempstead. Its owners, Carly and Andy, are keen to tackle an eyesore dating back to the Second World War

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 4x100m gold medallist Mark Lewis-Francis, swimming great Rebecca Adlington and Scottish internationals in football and rugby union respectively Charlie Adam and John Barclay join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A broken Billy snaps at Jay, and tells Lola about Jay and Honey - leaving his granddaughter in his bad books when he realises she already knew. Gray is happy to see how well Whitney is fitting in to her role and offers her a proposition, while Chelsea and Lucas wait for Jack

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Miles Jupp and Bob Mortimer and actresses Samantha Morton and Sarah Hadland join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from University with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Sam Neill, who is appearing in new Australian comedy-drama Rams, and actress Billie Piper, who talks about her film Rare Beasts, which she wrote, directed and stars in. Cold Feet star James Nesbitt discusses his new Northern Ireland-set BBC series Bloodlands, while comedian Ando Osho promotes her debut novel Asking for a Friend. Plus, music legend Tom Jones chats and performs his new single No Hole in My Head

programme.title: Young Guns (1988)
programme.desc: Petty crook Billy the Kid is taken in by English farmer John Tunstall and given a job as a ranch hand. However, when Tunstall is murdered by a business rival, Billy and the rest of his 'regulators' set out to avenge his death, leading them into conflict with the law - and marking the start of Billy's notoriety as a gunslinger. Western, with Emilio Estevez, Charlie Sheen, Kiefer Sutherland and Lou Diamond Phillips

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Gary Barlow, while chefs Angela Hartnett and Olia Hercules prepare dishes in the studio. Plus, archive clips and wine choices

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Fowey Estuary, one of Cornwall's hidden secrets, joining a friend on a boat trip to learn about the history of the area and getting a unique view of the buildings along the riverbank. He discovers the area's history of piracy, meets a family that make award-winning vodka, and makes a pancake with a wild blackberry compote and Cornish clotted cream

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Arsenal v Manchester United and West Ham United v Liverpool

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: West Bromwich Albion v Fulham (Kick-off 3.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at The Hawthorns. Both teams were promoted last season and are embroiled in a relegation battle this time around, with West Brom dismissing Slaven Bilic in December and turning to survival expert Sam Allardyce in their hopes of avoiding the drop. November's reverse fixture saw Fulham prevail 2-0 courtesy of first-half strikes by Bobby Decordova-Reid and Ola Aina

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, Ore Oduba and Kelvin Fletcher take on Reverends Kate Bottley and Richard Coles

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special drama edition general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson, Julia Brown, Arthur Darvill, Philip Franks, Cathy Tyson, Trudie Goodwin and Cathy Shipton

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Salazar's Revenge (2017)
programme.desc: Deadly ghost scavengers, led by Captain Salazar, escape from the Devil's Triangle, determined to kill every pirate at sea, including Captain Jack Sparrow. Captain Jack's only hope of survival lies in seeking out the legendary Trident of Poseidon, a powerful artefact that bestows upon its possessor total control over the seas. Swashbuckling sequel, starring Johnny Depp, Javier Bardem and Geoffrey Rush

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob's life is thrown into peril when a suspected bomber enters the emergency department, and an exhausted Marty struggles to accept his father's attempts at reconciliation. Medical drama, starring Charles Venn and Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The doting mother offers Winnie and Sharon a place to stay when the pipes in their house burst, but Agnes and Cathy soon discover that living with best friends isn't as much fun as they thought it would be. As if that wasn't enough for Agnes to cope with, she is asked by Father Damian to do a eulogy for a neighbour she never knew and there's Bono's 13th birthday party to host as well

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Manchester United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester City v Sheffield United at Etihad Stadium. The Gunners are looking to complete a double over United after prevailing 1-0 at Old Trafford in November, while City prevailed by the same scoreline when they met the Blades at Bramall Lane. Plus, Southampton v Aston Villa, Everton v Newcastle United, Chelsea v Burnley, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Bromwich Albion v Fulham

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of last weekend's AFC and NFC Championship matches

programme.title: The Goob (2014)
programme.desc: A teenager spends every summer helping out in his mother's cafe and working on the harvest in the nearby pumpkin fields. The latest harvest season proves a very different one, as tension brews with his mother's new boyfriend and a flirtatious relationship with a foreign worker in the fields makes him consider a different future. Drama, starring Liam Walpole and Marama Corlett

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Manchester United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester City v Sheffield United at Etihad Stadium. The Gunners are looking to complete a double over United after prevailing 1-0 at Old Trafford in November, while City prevailed by the same scoreline when they met the Blades at Bramall Lane. Plus, Southampton v Aston Villa, Everton v Newcastle United, Chelsea v Burnley, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Bromwich Albion v Fulham

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle return to Longleat Safari Park, where they get ready to welcome five Southern koalas that are en route from Adelaide in Australia. Jean Johansson is in the wolf wood to see if the European grey wolves can live up to their reputation for having one of the best senses of smell in the animal world

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Caroline Hawley help two teams scour an antiques fair on the island of Anglesey, while host Eric Knowles makes flour at a restored water mill in Bunbury, Cheshire

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser hosts the semi-final of the Gospel Singer of the Year, with judges Alexandra Burke, David Grant and Heather Small deciding who will make it to next week's final

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Appeal on behalf of Ovarian Cancer Action, a charity campaigning to increase awareness and fund scientific research into the disease

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: With three items saved from an Altrincham skip, Sarah Moore spruces up a table, Guy Trench helps an old vice find a new home, and Daniel Heath put his twist on a pair of chairs

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Ende's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how Earth's five oceans connect to form the largest ecosystem on the planet, and how its network of currents supports the health of the seas and marine wildlife. Cameras follow vast super-pods of dolphins and the flocks of gannets that follow them as they scour the oceans searching for food, as well as the tiny yet dazzling flamboyant cuttlefish that's looking for a mate

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison discovers how communities on her home turf of Gloucestershire are pulling together to one another during the pandemic. She meets the producers on a mission to get everyone eating local and healthy meals at affordable prices, finds out why gardening is on prescription, and helps out with the endangered hedgehog, which is facing a precarious future. Wildlife film-maker Jack Perks gets up close to dippers, Adam Henson sizes up a new boar for his pigs and Charlotte Smith investigates what future trade deals could mean for the food on the nation's plates

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Newby Hall & Gardens in Ripon, North Yorkshire. Treasures include an intriguing letter from Lord Nelson, politely replying to his optician just weeks before the Battle of Trafalgar. There's also a piece of the set from Live Aid in 1985, rescued from a skip, and an engagement ring picked up in a pawn shop. The Bradford Museum also launches an appeal to find the families related to thousands of photos in their archive

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Herman's behaviour becomes increasingly erratic and he is sent on compulsory leave. Will a private inspection of Kanit House bring him, Angela and their colleagues the major breakthrough they crave? Charles, meanwhile, is in Karachi attempting to set up business in Europe and as he attempts to snare a buyer for his gems, he faces up to memories of his past, while Marie-Andree and Ajay vie for ascendancy within the group

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring West Ham United v Liverpool at London Stadium, Leicester City v Leeds United at King Power Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at Amex Stadium. Liverpool, Leicester and Spurs will all hold aspirations of winning the league in what promises to be a closely fought and unpredictable title-race. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur, Everton v Manchester United and Aston Villa v Arsenal

programme.title: The Bling Ring (2013)
programme.desc: Five teenagers united by an obsession with celebrity steal from the houses of the rich and famous stars they idolise, using the internet to follow their targets' movements. When the police finally catch up with them, the arrest brings a taste of the fame they have craved. Fact-based drama, starring Emma Watson and Israel Broussard

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with former Olympic diver Ben Swain, who hoped he and his wife could make a fresh start in Australia after an injury cut short his sporting career

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The documentary follows three women attending Stroud Farmers' Market to sell their produce. Sisters Rowan and Lily took over their mother's business creating essential oils from plants grown in their garden. Now they are learning the ropes while trying to keep the business afloat. Meanwhile, dairy farmer Jess is selling produce from her cows - who she considers to be her 'business partners'. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Dominic Chinea undertakes the challenging restoration of a Carrom board, a traditional Asian board game, while Steven Kember teams up with silversmith Brenton West to repair a precious silver menorah. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher restores a small wooden duck once used in magic tricks, and bookbinder Christopher Shaw repairs a pair of photo albums charting Captain Scott's Antarctic expedition

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Feelgood Friday presented by Gethin Jones

programme.title: The Human Body: Secrets of Your Life Revealed
programme.desc: Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore the processes keeping human beings alive, seeing how powerful reflexes of fear and disgust keep people safe from danger and revealing the mechanisms bodies call on to repair damage. Xand visits one of the most advanced laboratories in the world to test his body's ability to maintain its inner balance, while Chris learns how scientists in Stockholm are trying to help people with phobias by investigating the neural mechanisms involved in fear and ways to control it

programme.title: The Human Body: Secrets of Your Life Revealed
programme.desc: Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore the way experiences shape the mind and body on the journey from being the one of the most helpless organisms on Earth to the most sophisticated. The brothers examine how people develop new skills - whether that's riding a bike or learning to walk - and reveal the unexpected link between the heartbeat and the ability to talk, while also showing how memories are formed in the brain and how experiences continue to change the body and genes throughout a lifetime

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents another selection of recipes from TV chefs, including the likes of James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower sets out to rescue the pups from the dangers of Zappa territory, but it is up to Mitch to complete the mission. Meanwhile, Mozart and Carlos make an appearance, now the leaders of their own groups. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Powderham Castle in Devon, antiques experts Will Axon and Mark Stacey find several treasures to take off to auction, including a shotgun case that belonged to a First World War hero and a pristine collection of Dinky toys. Plus, Paul Martin delves into the past of the castle, which has been home to the same family for more than 600 years, and uncovers a scandal that changed the course of that family's history

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns reflect on the lessons learned through the series, and how corner shops can continue to thrive in the changing world of 21st-century business. The programme examines how shops have reinvented themselves to compete with mini-supermarkets, the rise of zero-waste shops, and how other businesses, like cafes, coffee shops and micro pubs, are filling the role of community hub

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Megan McCubbin joins Chris Packham in the New Forest, getting a final look at the animals they've been witnessing for the last couple of weeks. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some amazing sights and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver Project in Cornwall

programme.title: The Investigation
programme.desc: With the volunteers and divers continuing their search for evidence, Jens explains to Jakob that it is very difficult without the exact trace of the route taken by the submarine. Soren Malling stars in this drama based on a true story. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: The Investigation
programme.desc: Prompted by Joachim, Jens asks the Swedish police to borrow their cadaver dogs, not commonly used in Denmark. Jakob is successful in procuring a warrant from the court to search the suspect's hard drives and a chance meeting brings the involvement of Torben Vang, an oceanographer with expertise on the complex currents in the waters between Denmark and Sweden. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Crossing the Sea of the Hebrides, Paul Murton heads to the Isle of Ewe, and on to the Summer Isles

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam, the BBC's Economics Editor, reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sharon helps Admiral Adama lead a daring rescue mission without the support of Apollo, and Tyrol rushes to save Cally from execution. With Edward James Olmos, Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. The crew forms an alliance with insurgents of New Caprica, forcing the Cylons to take drastic steps as they begin to lose control in the battle. Lee struggles to protect the civilian fleet on its journey toward Earth. Starring Edward James Olmos and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: I Remember You (2017)
programme.desc: After a number of strange deaths in the area, a new psychiatrist discovers one of the deceased was obsessed with the disappearance of his 8-year-old son three years previously. Meanwhile, three city dwellers are restoring a house when inexplicable, supernatural events start to occur. It becomes clear that the answer to both of these mysteries lie with the disappearance of a boy 60 years earlier. Icelandic mystery horror, starring Johannes Haukur Johannesson and Elma Stefania Agustsdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Welcome to the first ever Marrying Mum and Dad awards ceremony - the 'I do's'. The families from series 4 celebrate their best moments. But who will be crowned 'Most Terrific Transport' or 'Most Outrageous Outfit'? Time to relive all the amazing highs from last series weddings

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield look back at all the amazing dens built throughout the series and reminisce the big reveals

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Chef Omari makes his twist on everyone's favourite - spaghetti bolognese. His luscious lentil bolognese is packed with healthy grains, vegetables and spice. It's the perfect dish for his family to chill out with during their official lazy day

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Tall grasses and beautiful flowers flourish as heavy rains leave the Kalahari covered with lush foliage. Although life is looking good for the Whiskers, the unusual conditions could spell disaster for the family. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart's unborn pups face an uncertain fate, and the mum-to-be assesses her options as she tries to avoid losing her second litter of the year. Meanwhile, Flower's newest arrivals are welcomed into the family

programme.title: Big Cats About the House
programme.desc: Giles Clark is feeling overwhelmed by the enormity of the task he has taken on with the Big Cat Sanctuary in Kent. He is paying close attention to the health of Maya the jaguar, who is now seven weeks old, in particular her wavering eyesight and back legs that still seem a little wobbly. Giles also visits Costa Rica to help jaguars in the wild

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to the last of the 16 dog groomers. First they tackle a quartet of cute Pomeranians, having to achieve a perfect pom-pom ball shape using scissors only. Then four party pooches hit the Dogwalk as a family of poodles and a Yorkshire terrier strut their stuff, having been given a makeover by the contestants. Who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor choose to go through to the quarter-finals?

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don travels to South America, home to more than 50,000 endemic plant species. Beginning in Brazil, the intrepid horticulturist views the private oasis of artist Burle Marx, and takes a trip by boat to the unique floating gardens of the Amazon, before visiting the coast of Chile - where an agriculturalist has created a landscape in tune with its craggy locale

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts a star-studded version of the culinary contest, which sees Mary Berry, Chris Bavin and Angela Hartnett judging 10 famous cooks. Taking part are Ferne McCann, Gareth Thomas, Desiree Burch, Ed Balls, Karim Zeroual, Ruth Madeley, Tom Read Wilson, Shobna Gulati, Ed Byrne and Rachel Johnson. In this first edition, Mary wants to know what home cooking means to the contestants, so she challenges them to produce their ultimate home-cooked dinner

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Joan of Arc (1948)
programme.desc: Multi-Oscar-nominated but ponderously paced historical drama adapted from Maxwell Anderson's stage play, charting the last battle undertaken by the medieval heroine of Orleans. Starring Ingrid Bergman, Jose Ferrer and George Coulouris

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by Swedish Academy Award-winning actress Ingrid Bergman, exploring the milestones of her life and career, including her appearances in Casablanca and Spellbound. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Two antiques experts hunt for antiques in the Bowes Museum at Barnard Castle in Co Durham. The venue was purpose-built to house the collection of Josephine and John Bowes. Presented by Paul Martin

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The group enjoy the final week of their experiment in Jaipur and attend one of the highlights of the Hindu calendar, the Ganesh Chaturthi festival, a riot of colour and noise, packed with people, floats and elephants. They also make a trip to Varanasi, one of the holiest cities in India, where Hindus come to be purified by the waters of the Ganges and to cremate their dead in the hope they have the best chance of reaching Nirvana. Back in Jaipur, Bobby and Jan go in search of property, with Bobby keen to find somewhere to buy and Jan falling in love with the idea of renting an apartment in a haveli among a local family

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tennis coach Judy Murray and former British number one Greg Rusedski compete, with expert guidance coming from Angus Ashworth and Raj Bisram

programme.title: Stormborn
programme.desc: Ewan McGregor narrates a documentary following animals surviving and thriving in countries on the northern edges of the Atlantic Ocean, beginning as winter releases its grip. A single Arctic fox scurries across the mountains of Iceland on a mission to re-unite with its mate from last year, while on Shetland an otter has successfully raised twin cubs to the brink of independence

programme.title: Comedy Connections
programme.desc: The secrets behind the comedy Dad's Army, the popular series set among the Home Guard of World War Two, which featured many veterans of stage and screen and established the successful writing partnership of Jimmy Perry and David Croft. The programme reveals the origins of the series title, Arthur Lowe's reluctance to learn his lines and how the pilot episode received a negative response from the test-audience

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Lance Corporal Jones finally takes the plunge as he decides to make an honest woman of Mrs Fox. Last-ever episode of the wartime comedy, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier, Clive Dunn, John Laurie and Ian Lavender. First shown in 1977

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig quizzes the panel on a few revolutionary ideas, with Alan Davies, Gyles Brandreth, Susan Calman and Jessica Fostekew going round the houses trying to come up with a decent answer

programme.title: Beautiful Boy (2018)
programme.desc: A New York Times writer worries when his son goes missing - but when he turns up two days later showing obvious signs of drug use, it proves to be just the first step on a journey through addiction, relapse and recovery that will last many years and impact all the family's lives. Drama based on the best-selling pair of memoirs by father and son David and Nic Sheff, starring Steve Carell and Timothée Chalamet

programme.title: The Conversation (1974)
programme.desc: An obsessive, paranoid electronic surveillance expert - renowned within the profession as being the best - makes the unprofessional mistake of getting personally involved in a dangerous case on his San Francisco patch. Francis Ford Coppola's thriller, starring Gene Hackman, John Cazale, Robert Duvall, Allen Garfield and Harrison Ford

programme.title: Look Back in Anger (1959)
programme.desc: A disillusioned cynic takes out his anger at the world by having an affair with his wife's best friend, causing untold misery for everyone he knows. Tony Richardson's adaptation of John Osborne's play, starring Richard Burton, Mary Ure and Claire Bloom

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Gill Johnson was convinced Australia would be the perfect place for her and her family to settle down. Husband Mark, however, was less than enthusiastic. Would a week in Perth persuade him that she wasn't just on a flight of fancy? Three years since their trial week, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they made the move Gill so desperately wanted

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden explores the gardens of England's northernmost county Northumberland, and participates in tree-planting at Cragside, where she meets 'fern queen' Jennifer. The horticulturist also visits Holy Island and hears how over-exuberant Edwardian planting is undermining the castle foundations. Plus, the story of engineer William Armstrong

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Winter returns and an abandoned Mozart tries to look after her newborn pups alone. Meanwhile, the rival Commandos force a confrontation with the Whiskers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower leads the Whiskers to better hunting grounds. However, the gang strays into the territory of the rival Zappa clan, and the ensuing battle leaves them searching for a missing pup

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower sets out to rescue the pups from the dangers of Zappa territory, but it is up to Mitch to complete the mission. Meanwhile, Mozart and Carlos make an appearance, now the leaders of their own groups. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is staying close to home exploring the Chilterns landscape and the people and skills that have shaped this Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. He discovers some hidden historical gems revealed by a revolutionary new mapping technique, heads deep into the woods to learn about the ancient art of bodging and rolls his sleeves up to help clean up ponds which are so desperately needed by wildlife. Joe Crowley investigates if large- scale schemes such as HS2 tally with the Government's pledge to go green, while Adam Henson calls in a horse whisperer to help with a nervous Exmoor pony

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: On the wild north Atlantic coast, Rick heads out to sea to go fishing for lobsters with his old friend and celebrity chef Nathan Outlaw, before helping to cook up a lobster risotto. Later, at Tresillian House near Newquay, Rick meets with head gardener John Harris to find out more about his Victorian walled garden

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: Le Mans (1971)
programme.desc: A racing driver battles against the odds to recover from a serious injury, determined to take on his arch rival in the prestigious 24-hour competition - and win a widow's heart. Drama, starring Steve McQueen, Elga Andersen, Ronald Leigh-Hunt and Christopher Waite

programme.title: The Train (1964)
programme.desc: As the Allies look set to liberate Paris from the Nazi forces, the Germans resolve to take all they can from the city - and plot to transport national treasures back to Berlin. However, bold French Resistance forces vow to stop them, enlisting the help of a train inspector along the way. Second World War adventure, starring Burt Lancaster, Paul Scofield, Jeanne Moreau and Michel Simon

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the night slalom from Schladming in Austria, which did not have the usual 50,000 fans present, but still featured the best technical skiers in the world. Plus, highlights of the women's downhill from the treacherous Kandahar course in Garmisch-Partenkirchen, Germany

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The documentary follows the Toms as they try to resurrect the last boat built in Looe and invest in the future of the town's fishing fleet, with three generations throwing themselves into the fray. The Toms' journey to get the boat back out and fishing takes them through the challenges of Britain's quota system, in which just five families own nearly a third of the country's entire fish quota

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The globetrotting presenter recalls some of the most dangerous experiences of his travels, from coming under fire in war-torn Mogadishu to squaring off with a female wrestler in Mexico City. Among the past destinations featured are a police cell in the far east of Russia, and the programme also looks back at his encounters with ambulance crews in San Salvador and anti-smuggling police in Italy

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh heads to more countries that are rarely visited by tourists to get a sense of the local culture and find out if they deserve their reputation. In this first edition, he heads to Zimbabwe in the aftermath of Robert Mugabe's rule, to discover whether British people should be holidaying in the southern African country once again. He meets Chipo, a teacher who was born and raised in Harare under Mugabe's rule. She explains that although people have been coming to the country, few venture beyond Victoria Falls. She wants to show Rom what they are missing on a grand, epic road trip that takes in National Parks and close-up encounters with rhinos, elephants and the so-called 'cheetah ambassador'

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert: Stand Up to Infertility
programme.desc: With he and his wife struggling to conceive, the comedian investigates male infertility, setting out to discover why nobody seems to be addressing the issue despite it accounting for around 50 per cent of all fertility cases. He discovers that the fact men don't want to talk about infertility is doing just as much harm as the condition itself

programme.title: Lance
programme.desc: Part two of two. A personal examination of the rise and fall of Lance Armstrong. The series examines the young Texan cyclist's rise to fame, his struggle with testicular cancer and subsequent emergence as a global icon with seven consecutive Tour de France titles, and his eventual downfall when he was revealed as being at the centre of one of the biggest doping scandals in sporting history

programme.title: The Homesman (2014)
programme.desc: A single woman living in a frontier town is asked by her church to escort three women traumatised by the hardships of their lives to a safe refuge in the East. Along the way, she saves an outlaw's life and persuades him to help her protect her charges on the perilous journey through the wilderness. Western based on the novel by Glendon Swarthout, directed by and starring Tommy Lee Jones, with Hilary Swank, Meryl Streep and Miranda Otto

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Matt uses his powerful form of interrogation to extract the truth from hostage Noah, who reveals how he became involved with Nathan's government plot to capture all the heroes. The mind-reader's discoveries then bring Peter face to face with the Hunter. Fantasy drama, guest starring Zeljko Ivanek, with Greg Grunberg, Jack Coleman and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Using a tip-off from Rebel, Matt and Peter head to Building 26 to rescue Daphne and recover files that will expose the government's nefarious plans. Meanwhile, Claire finds help from an unlikely source in smuggling Alex out of the city, Sylar is led to the truth about his mother and the Hunter puts his explosive plot into action. Greg Grunberg, Milo Ventimiglia and Hayden Panettiere star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac must fight for control of the hospital when a returning patient wreaks havoc. Reeling from Skylar's tragedy, the F1s try to cover their tracks when a flirtatious patient makes a serious accusation. Grieving Sacha is scared of letting go of his daughter while a desperate Jodie is on the loose, and his usual arsenal of lies, delays and deceptions may not be enough to keep Beka safe

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The presenter explores how a new generation of composers transformed musical theatre by embracing more gritty, challenging subjects, from the mean streets of 1950s New York in West Side Story to the Dickensian London of British blockbuster Oliver! Neil learns the stories behind Broadway hits Fiddler on the Roof and A Chorus Line, and celebrates the ground-breaking work of composer and lyricist Stephen Sondheim. He also reveals the 'secrets' of some classic numbers with the help of performers Robert Lindsay and Frances Ruffelle

programme.title: Cilla at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of memorable TV appearances by Cilla Black from the BBC archives. The much-missed performer began her presenting career in 1968 on the BBC, having already become one of Britain's most popular singers. The programme features her appearances on Not Only, But Also, The Ken Dodd Show, Top of the Pops and The Royal Variety Performance as well as her self-titled series, plus her famous 1973 duet with Marc Bolan

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on July 19, with performances by artists including Paul Young, Inspiral Carpets, Dream Warriors, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, Partners in Kryme, Diana Ross, Paul Young, FAB featuring MC Parker, Madonna, Elton John and Paula Abdul

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on July 26, with performances by artists including Blue Pearl, the Soup Dragons featuring Junior Reid, Paula Abdul, Technotronic featuring Ya Kid K, DNA featuring Suzanne Vega, MC Hammer, River City People and Partners in Kryme

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at groundbreaking hip-hop songs, revisited by the artists behind them, that were pivotal to the evolution of American music and culture. The first episode features Kanye West's Grammy-winning 2004 release Jesus Walks, which stood out by placing West's personal interpretation of Christian faith at the centre of its message

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at American rapper Kendrick Lamar's 2015 song Alright, which was inspired by a visit to South Africa. A song about hope amid personal struggles, it became a soundtrack adopted by Black Lives Matter

programme.title: Hip Hop at the BBC
programme.desc: Hip-hop performances through the decades from the BBC archives, including the Sugarhill Gang in 1979 and Run DMC, LL Cool J and Eric B & Rakim in the '80s. Plus, Ice T, Monie Love, Fugees and the Roots in the '90s and latterly Dr Dre featuring Eminem, Dizzee Rascal and Jay-Z

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Having spent over 25 years in the music industry, agent Emma Banks has worked with some of the world's most famous artists and seen first-hand the fine line between success and failure. The secret to success and stardom is an elusive formula of luck, timing talent as well as the team behind the talent - the record executives, label bosses and A&R gurus. Here, Emma offers a behind-the-scenes insight into how the industry finds talent and creates superstars. Contributors include Motown legend Martha Reeves, Blur's Alex James and Labelle's Nona Hendryx

programme.title: Cilla at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of memorable TV appearances by Cilla Black from the BBC archives. The much-missed performer began her presenting career in 1968 on the BBC, having already become one of Britain's most popular singers. The programme features her appearances on Not Only, But Also, The Ken Dodd Show, Top of the Pops and The Royal Variety Performance as well as her self-titled series, plus her famous 1973 duet with Marc Bolan

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The presenter explores how a new generation of composers transformed musical theatre by embracing more gritty, challenging subjects, from the mean streets of 1950s New York in West Side Story to the Dickensian London of British blockbuster Oliver! Neil learns the stories behind Broadway hits Fiddler on the Roof and A Chorus Line, and celebrates the ground-breaking work of composer and lyricist Stephen Sondheim. He also reveals the 'secrets' of some classic numbers with the help of performers Robert Lindsay and Frances Ruffelle

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn continue to relive the golden age of steam and how the Victorian railways created modern Britain. They visit Beamish in County Durham, learning how the rail companies first realised there was money to be made in moving people rather than commodities. Then Peter and Alex live the life of navvies, the men who built the thousands of miles of tracks in all weather conditions, and Ruth finds out what impact the new railroads had on the cottage industries. She also learns about the role of the guard, and the trio learn what precautions were necessary for taking a long journey on a train that was yet to include the necessary facilities

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The intrepid traveller embarks on a trip from the Khyber Pass to the Bay of Bengal, an arduous journey through Pakistan's mountains, India, Nepal, China and finally Bangladesh. The adventure begins on the Khyber steam railway, taking in Darra, where he browses for a unique firearm. He then heads toward Chitral to watch a freestyle polo grudge-match take place on the highest polo field in the world

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Josephine heads back to Paris with Youssef, but his brother Souleymane is now in grave danger, while Judge Vargas, who was in charge of Gilou's infiltration, decides to cut his losses. But what does this mean for Gilou?

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Last-minute changes on the day of the hold-up cause chaos and the police's meticulously planned intervention is thrown into disarray. As the situation becomes more and more dangerous, Laure does all she can to get Gilou out to safety, but her options are limited - and very risky. Final episode of the French crime drama, starring Caroline Proust

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on July 19, with performances by artists including Paul Young, Inspiral Carpets, Dream Warriors, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, Partners in Kryme, Diana Ross, Paul Young, FAB featuring MC Parker, Madonna, Elton John and Paula Abdul
