programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1890s, with cheap foodstuffs and mass-manufactured goods having found their way into the slum's shop - but only some of the residents can afford them. The Howarths' relative prosperity means Mandy can turn her attention to being a respectable Victorian house wife, while the Potters are offered a lifeline by their neighbour Maria, who needs help with her laundry business. Michael Mosley presents

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore uncovers the role of religion in creating and maintaining the power of the city. In the first episode, he explores how every event in ancient Rome revolved around worship and that nothing took place without calling on the Pantheon, the temple to their gods. He also reveals the part played by sacredness and sacrifice in Roman culture

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: The adventurer and his team make it across the Persian Gulf to Dubai, and catch a container ship to India. After soaking up the colours, smells and flavours of Mumbai, they endure a 19-hour train journey to Delhi, where they take part in a tuk-tuk race through the city. They then ride to Agra astride handmade classic motorbikes, but cameraman Mungo seriously injures his knee and is forced to return home

programme.title: West Side Stories - The Making of a Classic
programme.desc: West Side Story is one of the best-loved musicals of all time, with its timeless story and exhilarating dance and music continuing to excite audiences around the globe. Songs such as Maria, Somewhere, Tonight and America having become some of the biggest hits in showbusiness and yet the musical had an uneasy birth and was even turned away by producers when it was first put together in the 1950s. In this documentary, Strictly Come Dancing judge Bruno Tonioli and Radio 3 presenter Suzy Klein examine the origins of West Side Story, which brought together the talents of Arthur Laurents, Leonard Bernstein, Stephen Sondheim and Jerome Robbins

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: With a career spanning 50 years and a catalogue of hits to his name including Cats, Les Miserables, Phantom of the Opera and Miss Saigon, Cameron Mackintosh is about to launch hit US musical Hamilton in London. Alan Yentob meets the impresario to discover how a timber merchant's son became the most successful man in musical theatre and in the process changed the face and sound of the business across the globe

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City

programme.desc: The Line draws a selection of animals - but none of them makes the right noises

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Flamingo and John are invited to Sarah and Duck's house, but a big obstacle lies in store for John

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Charlie wins first prize at school for his home-made rocket, which he made from 10 yoghurt pots and some extra wide tin foil. As he puts his rocket safely away on the top shelf he explains to Lola that the rocket really isn't a toy and she must promise not to touch it or play with it. Then Lola and her imaginary friend, Soren Lorenson, find that they have to transport an elephant back to the jungle, so Soren persuades Lola that using the rocket might be a good idea. Needless to say, the duo soon break the treasured possession

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: It seems all of Tee's friends have the brand new Mega Action Ape toy. Tee is desperate for one too, but his birthday isn't for ages. However, anything is possible when Mo gets creative

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A special star passes over the planet that is meant to make the star roses bloom, but the flowers fail to appear

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy comes home to find Igglepiggle has gone to sleep in her bed

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Show Me Show Me
programme.desc: Chris Jarvis and Pui Fan Lee cannot stop throwing confetti. When there is far too much to sweep up, Miss Mouse comes to the rescue

programme.title: Baby Jake
programme.desc: The adventurous infant joins the Hamsternauts on a trip into space, and takes part in a game of musical statues

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po do a roundy round dance and watch children in Tummy Tales ride a carousel which goes round and round too

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Topsy reads Zebby a bedtime story, but Raa Raa insists on making noises to accompany the tale, leaving his friend unable to get off to sleep

programme.title: Postman Pat: SDS
programme.desc: The sound of a digeridoo helps to uncover Dottie's hiding place when she goes missing

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula play a vooshing game with their favourite toys, Hoppity Voosh and Hippo Fair, and they throw them into the air to see how high they can fly

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Charlie upsets Bing when he doesn't want to play the games he planned, but the pair soon find that making a noise in the kitchen is lots of fun

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog puts up a bouncy castle to celebrate Tag's birthday

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels go for a walk in the woods to help Duggee look for a special kind of leaf

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters have been invited by the Korowai tribe to visit their village in the rainforest of Papua in Indonesia

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The rabbits discover a hoard of much needed winter vegetables in Mr McGregor's garden, and build a spectacular new sled to transport the stash home

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts and a group of crabs find themselves caught in the middle of a feud between two sets of anemones

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb makes a tractor out of a cereal box and parades it around the nursery

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee wakes up super-early and wants to play immediately. Mo wonders how much zooming is needed before everybody needs a nap

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: When a snow storm comes to Chuggington, Wilson and Brewster get caught out in the cold and must work together to make their way home.

programme.title: The Furchester Hotel
programme.desc: A parrot copies every woof, miaow and monster noise of the other guests, so the Furchesters try to calm the chaos

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Nick is looking at family photographs and creates a Popcast song about being a brother

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Jemima's new egg goes missing, and only Peter Rabbit is able to put together the clues to find the culprit - but must find the egg before it hatches

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Pre-school live action series featuring a cast of youngsters pretending to be grown-ups in an imaginary town. Narrated by Eamonn Holmes

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Things turn messy when Robert invents a cake-making machine. Guest starring Katy Ashworth, with Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to Brazil and meets a troop of capuchin monkeys who lead him to some honey

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a great place to draw some pictures using charcoal.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle has so much fun on a doggy playdate at Mr Willow's house that he decides to stay for a sleepover. However, the Brooklyn-Bells miss Waffle and cannot wait for his return. With the voice of Rufus Hound

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: When Bob mixes up a delivery of cakes with a delivery of fish, Mrs Juniper is forced to sell the fish. James and the kids help Mrs Juniper turn her cafe into a fish shop

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Dad takes the twins to the dentist for a check-up and, after hearing about the tooth fairy from their friend Josie, Topsy comes up with a plan to catch a glimpse of her

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Zak has been causing mayhem with his pretend 'golf stick'

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: The veteran seafarer recounts the story of how the Rainbow got her stripes. Bernard Cribbins stars

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley visits a deer farm in Scotland, while JB Gill helps harvest some winter vegetables

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In this programme we meet three children enjoying a very busy day.In Italy, seven-year-old Mario makes a birthday card for his dad - with a beautiful poem. In India, six-year-old Anushka goes on a journey to meet her family in the countryside. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's seven, makes a kite with his family - and flies it from their roof. In each film we see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and challenges of childhood are universal

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo always has something on his mind, so Lily and Felix help him find out how his thought processes work

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Isla and Noah make paper pirate boats, complete with flags. However, they need some water to float them, so it's off to the park with Mum and Dad

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne meets a vet who examines a pony's sore hoof, and Rory meets a vet and her Polar patient

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day looks at baby animals tricky first steps. Polar bear cubs slip on the arctic snow. In Africa a baby elephant needs mum's help to stand up. Mandarin ducklings face a huge leap from their tree trunk nest in Russia

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Tib suffers some serious misfortune, and it becomes clear it is all down to a sad chap called Bad Luck. Happily, Gigglegoon is on hand to turn their luck around through laughter

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is counting fish with Fisherman Tumble, while Justin and his friends are counting animals at a wildlife park

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Maddie takes us on a journey into space to explore planets, astronauts and rockets.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug meet Little Red Riding Hood and the Big Bad Wolf.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When the auditions for Mr Jeremy Fisher's annual recital attract the attention of Mr Tod, it's up to Peter to save the day with some musical mischief of his own

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: A photo of Gran Gran and JoJo's mummy when she was a little girl inspires JoJo and Gran Gran to take a walk and compare old photos of their neighbourhood to see how it has changed over time.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan visits Jess and Toby who are about to adopt some new pets from an animal shelter. They meet Marie from the shelter who talks to them about the responsibility of owning pets and together they decide to adopt two guinea pigs

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies' motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing emergency machines

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with spoons and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Tinpo and his team solve problems by asking, 'What if we tried this…?'

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: In an attempt to apologise to Kari for eating the cake she made him as a surprise, Barry tries to be kind in lots of different ways by becoming Captain Kind Kid

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: The story of how the vulture lost its feathers when it flew up to the sun

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo hears the announcement of a new Captain Chloe film on the radio, she's disheartened to learn that it won't be available to watch for some time. Luckily, Gran Gran has a Gran Gran Plan - she and JoJo can make their own film to tide them over, and Jared (a keen filmmaker) can help. JoJo learns all about the camera, acting and editing, eventually producing an amazing film trailer for the gang to watch with popcorn.

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Liam and Katie Morag choose special presents for Grannie Island on Grandmother's Day, but their plan to deliver them to her are threatened

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady takes to the skies in a knitted hot-air balloon to race against an old friend

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: A birthday surprise causes problems when Lola struggles to resist the temptation to tell Charlie about his present

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is trying to do some gardening, but Tee wants to hunt for treasure!

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A knitting needle ends up puncturing Granny's comfy puffball chair, so Major Clanger decides to make her a replacement

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos enjoy cleaning their teeth, while the Haahoos have their faces washed

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Celia Imrie reads A Busy Day for Birds

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones hosts the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin take a look around a shell of a property in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a bungalow in Lower Quinton, near Stratford-upon-Avon, and an end terrace in in Gorton, Manchester

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on how a detective's extraordinary ability to read body language brought a pair of international crooks to justice. Plus, the burglar who tried to batter his way into a chip shop and how inebriated night clubbers got a big shock when they took a short cut home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Phillip Serrell are on hand as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire. The competing duos then head to auction in Derbyshire. Presenter Charlie Ross oversees proceedings and also finds out about a classic car collection

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After the body of Reginald Brody is discovered at the bottom of Helmsley Tower, Chief Inspector Valentine returns to Kembleford to investigate. Suspecting that there is more to his death than suicide, Valentine enlists Father Brown to help his inquiries. Mark Williams and Hugo Speer star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reach Austria - the home of Mozart, the Sound of Music - on the fifth leg of their biking excursion across Europe. The trip starts in Vienna, where the pair unravel the mystery of the two competing Sachertorte recipes and taste the latest version - Sachertorte Reloaded

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Gill Johnson was convinced Australia would be the perfect place for her and her family to settle down. Husband Mark, however, was less than enthusiastic. Would a week in Perth persuade him that she wasn't just on a flight of fancy? Three years since their trial week, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they made the move Gill so desperately wanted

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two Scottish farming families hope to make healthy sales at the Bowhouse market, Fife. To try to squeeze more income from their milk, husband and wife Robert and Jane, along with their son Ben now make 27 tonnes of award-winning cheese each year on their 660-acre dairy farm. Meanwhile, the Bruces gave up careers to start a small farm growing botanicals to make into spirits and liquors. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes five more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers bid against each other on a vintage pub game, a small and mysterious statue and an apothecary set complete with tiny weights

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dirty Sanchez daredevil-turned-vegan chef Mathew Pritchard cooks a power-packed meal for members of the Cardiff Devils Ice hockey team, with the aim of supercharging their sticks and skates as they take on their sporting rivals

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 4x100m gold medallist Mark Lewis-Francis, swimming great Rebecca Adlington and Scottish internationals in football and rugby union respectively Charlie Adam and John Barclay join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A broken Billy snaps at Jay, and tells Lola about Jay and Honey - leaving his granddaughter in his bad books when he realises she already knew. Gray is happy to see how well Whitney is fitting in to her role and offers her a proposition, while Chelsea and Lucas wait for Jack

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Miles Jupp and Bob Mortimer and actresses Samantha Morton and Sarah Hadland join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from University with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Sam Neill, who is appearing in new Australian comedy-drama Rams, and actress Billie Piper, who talks about her film Rare Beasts, which she wrote, directed and stars in. Cold Feet star James Nesbitt discusses his new Northern Ireland-set BBC series Bloodlands, while comedian Ando Osho promotes her debut novel Asking for a Friend. Plus, music legend Tom Jones chats and performs his new single No Hole in My Head

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert: Stand Up to Infertility
programme.desc: With he and his wife struggling to conceive, the comedian investigates male infertility, setting out to discover why nobody seems to be addressing the issue despite it accounting for around 50 per cent of all fertility cases. He discovers that the fact men don't want to talk about infertility is doing just as much harm as the condition itself

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Gary Barlow, while chefs Angela Hartnett and Olia Hercules prepare dishes in the studio. Plus, archive clips and wine choices

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Fowey Estuary, one of Cornwall's hidden secrets, joining a friend on a boat trip to learn about the history of the area and getting a unique view of the buildings along the riverbank. He discovers the area's history of piracy, meets a family that make award-winning vodka, and makes a pancake with a wild blackberry compote and Cornish clotted cream

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Arsenal v Manchester United and West Ham United v Liverpool

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: West Bromwich Albion v Fulham (Kick-off 3.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at The Hawthorns. Both teams were promoted last season and are embroiled in a relegation battle this time around, with West Brom dismissing Slaven Bilic in December and turning to survival expert Sam Allardyce in their hopes of avoiding the drop. November's reverse fixture saw Fulham prevail 2-0 courtesy of first-half strikes by Bobby Decordova-Reid and Ola Aina

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, Ore Oduba and Kelvin Fletcher take on Reverends Kate Bottley and Richard Coles

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special drama edition general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson, Julia Brown, Arthur Darvill, Philip Franks, Cathy Tyson, Trudie Goodwin and Cathy Shipton

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Salazar's Revenge (2017)
programme.desc: Deadly ghost scavengers, led by Captain Salazar, escape from the Devil's Triangle, determined to kill every pirate at sea, including Captain Jack Sparrow. Captain Jack's only hope of survival lies in seeking out the legendary Trident of Poseidon, a powerful artefact that bestows upon its possessor total control over the seas. Swashbuckling sequel, starring Johnny Depp, Javier Bardem and Geoffrey Rush

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob's life is thrown into peril when a suspected bomber enters the emergency department, and an exhausted Marty struggles to accept his father's attempts at reconciliation. Medical drama, starring Charles Venn and Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The doting mother offers Winnie and Sharon a place to stay when the pipes in their house burst, but Agnes and Cathy soon discover that living with best friends isn't as much fun as they thought it would be. As if that wasn't enough for Agnes to cope with, she is asked by Father Damian to do a eulogy for a neighbour she never knew and there's Bono's 13th birthday party to host as well

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Manchester United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester City v Sheffield United at Etihad Stadium. The Gunners are looking to complete a double over United after prevailing 1-0 at Old Trafford in November, while City prevailed by the same scoreline when they met the Blades at Bramall Lane. Plus, Southampton v Aston Villa, Everton v Newcastle United, Chelsea v Burnley, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Bromwich Albion v Fulham

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of last weekend's AFC and NFC Championship matches

programme.title: The Goob (2014)
programme.desc: A teenager spends every summer helping out in his mother's cafe and working on the harvest in the nearby pumpkin fields. The latest harvest season proves a very different one, as tension brews with his mother's new boyfriend and a flirtatious relationship with a foreign worker in the fields makes him consider a different future. Drama, starring Liam Walpole and Marama Corlett

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Manchester United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester City v Sheffield United at Etihad Stadium. The Gunners are looking to complete a double over United after prevailing 1-0 at Old Trafford in November, while City prevailed by the same scoreline when they met the Blades at Bramall Lane. Plus, Southampton v Aston Villa, Everton v Newcastle United, Chelsea v Burnley, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Bromwich Albion v Fulham

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle return to Longleat Safari Park, where they get ready to welcome five Southern koalas that are en route from Adelaide in Australia. Jean Johansson is in the wolf wood to see if the European grey wolves can live up to their reputation for having one of the best senses of smell in the animal world

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Caroline Hawley help two teams scour an antiques fair on the island of Anglesey, while host Eric Knowles makes flour at a restored water mill in Bunbury, Cheshire

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser hosts the semi-final of the Gospel Singer of the Year, with judges Alexandra Burke, David Grant and Heather Small deciding who will make it to next week's final

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Appeal on behalf of Ovarian Cancer Action, a charity campaigning to increase awareness and fund scientific research into the disease

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: With three items saved from an Altrincham skip, Sarah Moore spruces up a table, Guy Trench helps an old vice find a new home, and Daniel Heath put his twist on a pair of chairs

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Ende's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway explores the foothills of the Cambrian Mountains and hikes around the Hafod Estate in Ceredigion. He then visits Laugharne on the Carmarthenshire coast, a favourite haunt of poet Dylan Thomas

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen explores the outer limits of the unknown, featuring tales of alien encounters, haunted houses and magical stones

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison discovers how communities on her home turf of Gloucestershire are pulling together to one another during the pandemic. She meets the producers on a mission to get everyone eating local and healthy meals at affordable prices, finds out why gardening is on prescription, and helps out with the endangered hedgehog, which is facing a precarious future. Wildlife film-maker Jack Perks gets up close to dippers, Adam Henson sizes up a new boar for his pigs and Charlotte Smith investigates what future trade deals could mean for the food on the nation's plates

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Newby Hall & Gardens in Ripon, North Yorkshire. Treasures include an intriguing letter from Lord Nelson, politely replying to his optician just weeks before the Battle of Trafalgar. There's also a piece of the set from Live Aid in 1985, rescued from a skip, and an engagement ring picked up in a pawn shop. The Bradford Museum also launches an appeal to find the families related to thousands of photos in their archive

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Herman's behaviour becomes increasingly erratic and he is sent on compulsory leave. Will a private inspection of Kanit House bring him, Angela and their colleagues the major breakthrough they crave? Charles, meanwhile, is in Karachi attempting to set up business in Europe and as he attempts to snare a buyer for his gems, he faces up to memories of his past, while Marie-Andree and Ajay vie for ascendancy within the group

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring West Ham United v Liverpool at London Stadium, Leicester City v Leeds United at King Power Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at Amex Stadium. Liverpool, Leicester and Spurs will all hold aspirations of winning the league in what promises to be a closely fought and unpredictable title-race. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur, Everton v Manchester United and Aston Villa v Arsenal

programme.title: The Bling Ring (2013)
programme.desc: Five teenagers united by an obsession with celebrity steal from the houses of the rich and famous stars they idolise, using the internet to follow their targets' movements. When the police finally catch up with them, the arrest brings a taste of the fame they have craved. Fact-based drama, starring Emma Watson and Israel Broussard

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on how a detective's extraordinary ability to read body language brought a pair of international crooks to justice. Plus, the burglar who tried to batter his way into a chip shop and how inebriated night clubbers got a big shock when they took a short cut home

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads to Somerset to help a couple with a £310,000 budget find a country home where character charm and a practical utility room are top of the wish list. Along the way, Ginny heads into the Somerset Levels to see how bird wildlife efforts have brought the endangered crane back from near extinction

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two Scottish farming families hope to make healthy sales at the Bowhouse market, Fife. To try to squeeze more income from their milk, husband and wife Robert and Jane, along with their son Ben now make 27 tonnes of award-winning cheese each year on their 660-acre dairy farm. Meanwhile, the Bruces gave up careers to start a small farm growing botanicals to make into spirits and liquors. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes five more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers bid against each other on a vintage pub game, a small and mysterious statue and an apothecary set complete with tiny weights

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Trying to ease the rising tensions, Levi is on a mission to make Bob his best buddy. Meanwhile, Amber is unimpressed by how Stevie has been running things in her absence, and Cameron needs to deflect Eve's growing suspicions

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 4x100m gold medallist Mark Lewis-Francis, swimming great Rebecca Adlington and Scottish internationals in football and rugby union respectively Charlie Adam and John Barclay join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A broken Billy snaps at Jay, and tells Lola about Jay and Honey - leaving his granddaughter in his bad books when he realises she already knew. Gray is happy to see how well Whitney is fitting in to her role and offers her a proposition, while Chelsea and Lucas wait for Jack

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Miles Jupp and Bob Mortimer and actresses Samantha Morton and Sarah Hadland join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from University with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Sam Neill, who is appearing in new Australian comedy-drama Rams, and actress Billie Piper, who talks about her film Rare Beasts, which she wrote, directed and stars in. Cold Feet star James Nesbitt discusses his new Northern Ireland-set BBC series Bloodlands, while comedian Ando Osho promotes her debut novel Asking for a Friend. Plus, music legend Tom Jones chats and performs his new single No Hole in My Head

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Joel Sked, Shaughan McGuigan, Craig Fowler and Robert Borthwick present a fun-filled look at Scottish football. It's Only Week X sees the team members present to their audience a bold claim that they have to demonstrate the cojones to back up and On the Fence sees them decide if a recognisable face from the world of Scottish football is for them or not and why

programme.title: Young Guns (1988)
programme.desc: Petty crook Billy the Kid is taken in by English farmer John Tunstall and given a job as a ranch hand. However, when Tunstall is murdered by a business rival, Billy and the rest of his 'regulators' set out to avenge his death, leading them into conflict with the law - and marking the start of Billy's notoriety as a gunslinger. Western, with Emilio Estevez, Charlie Sheen, Kiefer Sutherland and Lou Diamond Phillips

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Gary Barlow, while chefs Angela Hartnett and Olia Hercules prepare dishes in the studio. Plus, archive clips and wine choices

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Fowey Estuary, one of Cornwall's hidden secrets, joining a friend on a boat trip to learn about the history of the area and getting a unique view of the buildings along the riverbank. He discovers the area's history of piracy, meets a family that make award-winning vodka, and makes a pancake with a wild blackberry compote and Cornish clotted cream

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Arsenal v Manchester United and West Ham United v Liverpool

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: West Bromwich Albion v Fulham (Kick-off 3.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at The Hawthorns. Both teams were promoted last season and are embroiled in a relegation battle this time around, with West Brom dismissing Slaven Bilic in December and turning to survival expert Sam Allardyce in their hopes of avoiding the drop. November's reverse fixture saw Fulham prevail 2-0 courtesy of first-half strikes by Bobby Decordova-Reid and Ola Aina

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, Ore Oduba and Kelvin Fletcher take on Reverends Kate Bottley and Richard Coles

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special drama edition general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson, Julia Brown, Arthur Darvill, Philip Franks, Cathy Tyson, Trudie Goodwin and Cathy Shipton

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Salazar's Revenge (2017)
programme.desc: Deadly ghost scavengers, led by Captain Salazar, escape from the Devil's Triangle, determined to kill every pirate at sea, including Captain Jack Sparrow. Captain Jack's only hope of survival lies in seeking out the legendary Trident of Poseidon, a powerful artefact that bestows upon its possessor total control over the seas. Swashbuckling sequel, starring Johnny Depp, Javier Bardem and Geoffrey Rush

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob's life is thrown into peril when a suspected bomber enters the emergency department, and an exhausted Marty struggles to accept his father's attempts at reconciliation. Medical drama, starring Charles Venn and Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The doting mother offers Winnie and Sharon a place to stay when the pipes in their house burst, but Agnes and Cathy soon discover that living with best friends isn't as much fun as they thought it would be. As if that wasn't enough for Agnes to cope with, she is asked by Father Damian to do a eulogy for a neighbour she never knew and there's Bono's 13th birthday party to host as well

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Manchester United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester City v Sheffield United at Etihad Stadium. The Gunners are looking to complete a double over United after prevailing 1-0 at Old Trafford in November, while City prevailed by the same scoreline when they met the Blades at Bramall Lane. Plus, Southampton v Aston Villa, Everton v Newcastle United, Chelsea v Burnley, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Bromwich Albion v Fulham

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Action from the latest fixtures, featuring Celtic v St Mirren, Dundee United v Hibernian, Kilmarnock v St Johnstone and Livingston v Aberdeen

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of last weekend's AFC and NFC Championship matches

programme.title: The Goob (2014)
programme.desc: A teenager spends every summer helping out in his mother's cafe and working on the harvest in the nearby pumpkin fields. The latest harvest season proves a very different one, as tension brews with his mother's new boyfriend and a flirtatious relationship with a foreign worker in the fields makes him consider a different future. Drama, starring Liam Walpole and Marama Corlett

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Manchester United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester City v Sheffield United at Etihad Stadium. The Gunners are looking to complete a double over United after prevailing 1-0 at Old Trafford in November, while City prevailed by the same scoreline when they met the Blades at Bramall Lane. Plus, Southampton v Aston Villa, Everton v Newcastle United, Chelsea v Burnley, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Bromwich Albion v Fulham

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: A round-up of political news and events at Westminster and Holyrood.

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: The Very Rev Lorna Hood presents from the foyer of BBC Glasgow, while David Strachan interviews Harry Burns, former Chief Medical Officer for Scotland. Music is by Jarlath Henderson

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a house in Brierley Hill in the West Midlands, a former bank in Hyde, Greater Manchester, and a terraced home in Stanley Grove in London

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser hosts the semi-final of the Gospel Singer of the Year, with judges Alexandra Burke, David Grant and Heather Small deciding who will make it to next week's final

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Appeal on behalf of Ovarian Cancer Action, a charity campaigning to increase awareness and fund scientific research into the disease

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: With three items saved from an Altrincham skip, Sarah Moore spruces up a table, Guy Trench helps an old vice find a new home, and Daniel Heath put his twist on a pair of chairs

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Ende's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how Earth's five oceans connect to form the largest ecosystem on the planet, and how its network of currents supports the health of the seas and marine wildlife. Cameras follow vast super-pods of dolphins and the flocks of gannets that follow them as they scour the oceans searching for food, as well as the tiny yet dazzling flamboyant cuttlefish that's looking for a mate

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison discovers how communities on her home turf of Gloucestershire are pulling together to one another during the pandemic. She meets the producers on a mission to get everyone eating local and healthy meals at affordable prices, finds out why gardening is on prescription, and helps out with the endangered hedgehog, which is facing a precarious future. Wildlife film-maker Jack Perks gets up close to dippers, Adam Henson sizes up a new boar for his pigs and Charlotte Smith investigates what future trade deals could mean for the food on the nation's plates

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Newby Hall & Gardens in Ripon, North Yorkshire. Treasures include an intriguing letter from Lord Nelson, politely replying to his optician just weeks before the Battle of Trafalgar. There's also a piece of the set from Live Aid in 1985, rescued from a skip, and an engagement ring picked up in a pawn shop. The Bradford Museum also launches an appeal to find the families related to thousands of photos in their archive

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Herman's behaviour becomes increasingly erratic and he is sent on compulsory leave. Will a private inspection of Kanit House bring him, Angela and their colleagues the major breakthrough they crave? Charles, meanwhile, is in Karachi attempting to set up business in Europe and as he attempts to snare a buyer for his gems, he faces up to memories of his past, while Marie-Andree and Ajay vie for ascendancy within the group

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring West Ham United v Liverpool at London Stadium, Leicester City v Leeds United at King Power Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at Amex Stadium. Liverpool, Leicester and Spurs will all hold aspirations of winning the league in what promises to be a closely fought and unpredictable title-race. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur, Everton v Manchester United and Aston Villa v Arsenal

programme.title: The Bling Ring (2013)
programme.desc: Five teenagers united by an obsession with celebrity steal from the houses of the rich and famous stars they idolise, using the internet to follow their targets' movements. When the police finally catch up with them, the arrest brings a taste of the fame they have craved. Fact-based drama, starring Emma Watson and Israel Broussard

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones hosts the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin take a look around a shell of a property in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a bungalow in Lower Quinton, near Stratford-upon-Avon, and an end terrace in in Gorton, Manchester

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Gill Johnson was convinced Australia would be the perfect place for her and her family to settle down. Husband Mark, however, was less than enthusiastic. Would a week in Perth persuade him that she wasn't just on a flight of fancy? Three years since their trial week, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they made the move Gill so desperately wanted

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on how a detective's extraordinary ability to read body language brought a pair of international crooks to justice. Plus, the burglar who tried to batter his way into a chip shop and how inebriated night clubbers got a big shock when they took a short cut home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Phillip Serrell are on hand as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire. The competing duos then head to auction in Derbyshire. Presenter Charlie Ross oversees proceedings and also finds out about a classic car collection

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After the body of Reginald Brody is discovered at the bottom of Helmsley Tower, Chief Inspector Valentine returns to Kembleford to investigate. Suspecting that there is more to his death than suicide, Valentine enlists Father Brown to help his inquiries. Mark Williams and Hugo Speer star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reach Austria - the home of Mozart, the Sound of Music - on the fifth leg of their biking excursion across Europe. The trip starts in Vienna, where the pair unravel the mystery of the two competing Sachertorte recipes and taste the latest version - Sachertorte Reloaded

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich head to Hertfordshire to come up with design ideas for a large garden in Hemel Hempstead. Its owners, Carly and Andy, are keen to tackle an eyesore dating back to the Second World War

Will a private inspection of Kanit House bring him, Angela and their colleagues the major breakthrough they crave? Charles, meanwhile, is in Karachi attempting to set up business in Europe and as he attempts to snare a buyer for his gems, he faces up to memories of his past, while Marie-Andree and Ajay vie for ascendancy within the group

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring West Ham United v Liverpool at London Stadium, Leicester City v Leeds United at King Power Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at Amex Stadium. Liverpool, Leicester and Spurs will all hold aspirations of winning the league in what promises to be a closely fought and unpredictable title-race. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur, Everton v Manchester United and Aston Villa v Arsenal

programme.title: The Bling Ring (2013)
programme.desc: Five teenagers united by an obsession with celebrity steal from the houses of the rich and famous stars they idolise, using the internet to follow their targets' movements. When the police finally catch up with them, the arrest brings a taste of the fame they have craved. Fact-based drama, starring Emma Watson and Israel Broussard

programme.title: Do Black Lives Matter in Brazil?
programme.desc: In the year when Black Lives Matter became a rallying cry around the world, a BBC Brazil investigation discovered that in the first half of 2020, more people were killed by the police in the state of Rio de Janeiro state than in the whole of the USA. Of those killed, 75% were black Brazilians, many of whom were just teenagers. Ligia Guimaraes meets several families fighting for justice over the killing of their sons.

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: As remote medicine becomes more commonplace, Click looks at easy-to-use, medical-grade, diagnostic devices that are becoming available so family physicians can provide a full consultation remotely. The teams also look at a new breed of whiskies that are mixed and aged by AI.

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: As remote medicine becomes more commonplace, Click looks at easy-to-use, medical-grade, diagnostic devices that are becoming available so family physicians can provide a full consultation remotely. programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: After social media suspended Donald Trump, some have questioned the power of the Silicon Valley giants. Alexei Navalny and Angela Merkel, among others, have wondered whether it was a step too far. The big five of Facebook, Amazon, Apple, Google and Twitter may have had humble origins, but they're now among the biggest companies in the world, wielding enormous global influence. Has big tech gone too far with this kind of censorship, removing more content and threatening freedom of speech, or is its intervention long overdue? And in the age of these new media monoliths, do we want them to be mere platforms or must they accept that they're publishers, responsible for content and democratically accountable?

programme.desc: How the flying fish can defend themselves from their predators by flying through the water at speed

programme.desc: Tom arrives in time to help Twigs collect bark for his 'bark-book' - taking to the skies with the Kangaroo Spell to catch all that they need for his collection. However, their hard work is in vain when their collection is stolen

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Pàdraig's deliveries are delayed when a package of carwash rollers falls from the back of the van

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley

programme.title: Port Pàipeir

programme.title: Cuirm @ Celtic

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: Following the efforts being made by scientists, conservationists and local people in the Highlands as they work to repair the damage of the past and rebuild wild landscapes and habitats. The programme meets Dave Anderson from Forestry Commission Scotland and his colleague Simon Smith as they tag and monitor red kite and osprey chicks, gaining a unique perspective of the lives of the birds before they fly the nest. Cameras also capture the efforts of the Scottish Wildcat Action Plan team, as well as Paul Lister's project to reintroduce wolves and bears to his Alladale Wilderness Reserve

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Kilmarnock v St Johnstone. Full-match coverage of the top-flight encounter at Rugby Park. With commentary by Alex O'Henley

programme.title: ALT

programme.title: Judge Rinder

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which husband and wife Tony and Caroline from Bromley, south London, San Francisco-born Eric and his husband Russell, and childhood sweethearts Ruka and Ibrahim from Peckham, south London, use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Endeavour

The case brings back unpleasant memories for the detective, while his colleagues are thrown into disarray when Bright is also rushed to hospital. Morse becomes fixated with a series of mysterious deaths that have occurred on the ward, and tries to find the scientific explanation behind stories of a curse

programme.desc: Gran accidentally wins a board game as a Bingo prize, and after being locked up for years the game has a vendetta all of its own.

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Robin Booty Scootys his backside off, the Titans must go on an adventure in order to help Robin recover the lost booty

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Charley Boorman, who chats about his new series Long Way Up, and chefs Lenny Carr-Roberts and Ryan and Liam Simpson-Trotman, who share recipes for custard tart and venison. Plus, James goes on a food adventure to Devon

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares a collection of simple, rustic recipes that can be put together easily, as well as revealing the workings of the world-famous Le Manoir aux Quat'Saisons where he is Chef Patron and which provides much of the fruit and vegetables that go into his dishes. In the first episode, he is joined by Angela Hartnett, who will be cooking cep tagliatelle. Other recipes include meringue with blackcurrant coulis and a heritage salad with beetroot straight from Raymond's gardens

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef drives into New Orleans in a 1970s Pontiac, where he tries local specialities including the po boy sandwich and sweet beignets. James also visits the French Quarter and tries of one of the local cocktails

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Ed Chamberlin present coverage of the 3.35 Paddy Power Cleeve Hurdle Race from Cheltenham, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, races from 2.05, 2.40 and 3.15 from Doncaster. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the arcade-themed quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win the chance to scoop a £10,000 jackpot. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as parents-to-be Dale and Vicki, couple Chitchalena and Alistair, and brother and sister Chris and Joy use their powers of deduction to try to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with contestants Phil Daniels, Steph McGovern and AJ Odudu all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Last week saw Bush Baby was revealed to be actor and comedian John Thomson. Now the remaining seven mystery singers take to the stage once more, only for two of them to be eliminated and unmasked. Joel Dommett presents, with guest panellist Alan Carr joining Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the fifth round of this year's blind auditions, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The coaches will be judging tonight's contestants on their voice alone, listening out for that perfect performance that inspires them to spin their chairs

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Angela Griffin, Martin Kemp, Jessica Knappet and Jason Manford to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies (2014)
programme.desc: Dwarf king Thorin reclaims control of his homeland, but his descent into madness places him on the brink of war with his human and elven allies. As Thorin's Hobbit friend Bilbo tries to save the tormented ruler from himself, a vast horde of orcs gathers to destroy them all. Conclusion of the fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Martin Freeman, Richard Armitage, Ian McKellen, Luke Evans and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers compete in a series of challenges to win a £3,000 prize

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Dino Dana
programme.desc: Dad tries to get Dana up and ready for school, but she is busy dreaming about discovering the first T Rex fossil

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: If there were two things Dave could wish for it would be for Fuzzypeg to smell better, and for Gran to be nicer. Is getting what you want always a good thing?

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave gets a girlfriend who turns out to be an alien. And she turns out to bear a striking similarity to an everyday household item.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Kayo and John must stop the Chaos Crew from stealing a dangerous chemical from a global seed vault

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny goes extreme window cleaning, but will he be brave enough to abseil down the building and avoid the forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A local archaeology dig inspires Mr Bean to go hunting for treasure

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: When Tom drinks a potion given to him by a Gypsy woman and shrinks to the size of an insect he must beg Jerry Van Mouseling for help in finding a cure

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: When a priceless Dracula family heirloom is destroyed, the four kids are the prime suspects. And when no one will own up to it, Aunt Lydia puts them on trial to get to the truth

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Jessica Cruz and Pam Isley bond over protecting an ancient tree from being cut down.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia heads to Isles of Scilly, where she walks a five-mile circular loop around St Martins, an island renowned for its white sand beaches and azure seas. Later, she finishes her island adventure with a trip to Tresco, where she visits the Abbey Gardens, world famous for its sub-tropical plants

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh is joined by actor Samantha Bond and football presenter Jake Humphrey. There's also a couple of miniature rare breed pigs, a sample of the best in British red wine and David Domoney has a guide to carnivorous plants

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares a collection of simple, rustic recipes that can be put together easily, as well as revealing the workings of the world-famous Le Manoir aux Quat'Saisons where he is Chef Patron and which provides much of the fruit and vegetables that go into his dishes. In the first episode, he is joined by Angela Hartnett, who will be cooking cep tagliatelle. Other recipes include meringue with blackcurrant coulis and a heritage salad with beetroot straight from Raymond's gardens

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team head to Hythe, Hampshire to create a sensory outdoor space for a four-year-old girl with severely limited vision, who will one day go completely blind. The team transform the family's current bland garden into a safe family haven where little Isabelle can learn about the outside world in a comfortable environment

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter takes a 300-kilometre road trip along the stunning coast of the Western Cape, heading for Gansbaai, otherwise known as Shark Town. He stops for lunch at a South African fish and chip shop, releases rehabilitated African Penguins back into the wild, and braves a boat trip through shark-infested waters

programme.title: Kindergarten Cop (1990)
programme.desc: A tough detective is forced to pose as a preschool teacher in a bid to capture a drug dealer whose son is one of the pupils. However, his biggest challenge turns out to be controlling a class full of five-year-olds. Comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Penelope Ann Miller, Linda Hunt, Pamela Reed and Richard Tyson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Perry Fenwick, Jenny Eclair, Ore Oduba and Richard Madeley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: All 11 celebrities and their professional skating partners perform a routine from a famous musical in the hope of avoiding the dreaded skate off. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel, alongside dance supremo Ashley Banjo and all-round entertainer John Barrowman. Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Presented by Jeremy Clarkson

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Charlotte decides that she is ready to return to school, but Alice feels lost without her and struggles to cope. Before the inquest into Harry's death can go ahead, the police must identify the mysterious figure on the CCTV and, with George becoming increasingly evasive, Alice is forced to go behind his back to get the answers she needs

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son continue their European adventure in Switzerland, where they take a ride on a mountain rollercoaster before exercising their vocal chords when they try the Swiss tradition of yodelling. The following day, Barney arranges a unique way for them to enjoy some local cheese, and then drive to the Contra Dam, where Barney reveals they are about to attempt one of James Bond's most famous stunts

programme.title: The Chasers Road Trip: Trains, Brains and Automobiles
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty, Mark Labbett and Shaun Wallace - better known to The Chase fans as the Governess, the Beast and the Dark Destroyer - continue their investigation into different types of intelligence by going head to head with child geniuses in the UK. Along the way, they delve deeper into IQ, find out about the link between emotional intelligence and creativity, and learn what sleep can tell a person about their intelligence

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present two stories of people searching for missing relatives who were found much closer than they could ever have imagined. Pauline Pedder was looking for the daughter who she put up for adoption 50 years ago, while Donna Cowell wanted to find out what happened to her younger brother

programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Croft, featuring round 13 of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup and round 20 of the British F4 Championship

programme.title: Craig of the Creek

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the kids are once again waylaid, this time by mole rats that have deemed Bao their god.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Charley Boorman, who chats about his new series Long Way Up, and chefs Lenny Carr-Roberts and Ryan and Liam Simpson-Trotman, who share recipes for custard tart and venison. Plus, James goes on a food adventure to Devon

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares a collection of simple, rustic recipes that can be put together easily, as well as revealing the workings of the world-famous Le Manoir aux Quat'Saisons where he is Chef Patron and which provides much of the fruit and vegetables that go into his dishes. In the first episode, he is joined by Angela Hartnett, who will be cooking cep tagliatelle. Other recipes include meringue with blackcurrant coulis and a heritage salad with beetroot straight from Raymond's gardens

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef drives into New Orleans in a 1970s Pontiac, where he tries local specialities including the po boy sandwich and sweet beignets. James also visits the French Quarter and tries of one of the local cocktails

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Ed Chamberlin present coverage of the 3.35 Paddy Power Cleeve Hurdle Race from Cheltenham, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, races from 2.05, 2.40 and 3.15 from Doncaster. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the arcade-themed quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win the chance to scoop a £10,000 jackpot. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund

programme.title: Lingo

programme.title: The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies (2014)
programme.desc: Dwarf king Thorin reclaims control of his homeland, but his descent into madness places him on the brink of war with his human and elven allies. As Thorin's Hobbit friend Bilbo tries to save the tormented ruler from himself, a vast horde of orcs gathers to destroy them all. Conclusion of the fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Martin Freeman, Richard Armitage, Ian McKellen, Luke Evans and Orlando Bloom

There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.desc: Julia heads to Isles of Scilly, where she walks a five-mile circular loop around St Martins, an island renowned for its white sand beaches and azure seas. Later, she finishes her island adventure with a trip to Tresco, where she visits the Abbey Gardens, world famous for its sub-tropical plants

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares a collection of simple, rustic recipes that can be put together easily, as well as revealing the workings of the world-famous Le Manoir aux Quat'Saisons where he is Chef Patron and which provides much of the fruit and vegetables that go into his dishes. programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team head to Hythe, Hampshire to create a sensory outdoor space for a four-year-old girl with severely limited vision, who will one day go completely blind. The team transform the family's current bland garden into a safe family haven where little Isabelle can learn about the outside world in a comfortable environment

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter takes a 300-kilometre road trip along the stunning coast of the Western Cape, heading for Gansbaai, otherwise known as Shark Town. He stops for lunch at a South African fish and chip shop, releases rehabilitated African Penguins back into the wild, and braves a boat trip through shark-infested waters

programme.title: Kindergarten Cop (1990)
programme.desc: A tough detective is forced to pose as a preschool teacher in a bid to capture a drug dealer whose son is one of the pupils. However, his biggest challenge turns out to be controlling a class full of five-year-olds. Comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Penelope Ann Miller, Linda Hunt, Pamela Reed and Richard Tyson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: All 11 celebrities and their professional skating partners perform a routine from a famous musical in the hope of avoiding the dreaded skate off. programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

Before the inquest into Harry's death can go ahead, the police must identify the mysterious figure on the CCTV and, with George becoming increasingly evasive, Alice is forced to go behind his back to get the answers she needs

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son continue their European adventure in Switzerland, where they take a ride on a mountain rollercoaster before exercising their vocal chords when they try the Swiss tradition of yodelling. The following day, Barney arranges a unique way for them to enjoy some local cheese, and then drive to the Contra Dam, where Barney reveals they are about to attempt one of James Bond's most famous stunts

Along the way, they delve deeper into IQ, find out about the link between emotional intelligence and creativity, and learn what sleep can tell a person about their intelligence
